perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” shall be deemed to include any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, forecasts, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that derive from, contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives pursuant hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” shall be deemed to include any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, forecasts, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that derive from, contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the data furnished to the Claiming Party or its Representatives pursuant hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Referring to the 'Receiving Party' as the 'Claiming Party', when discussing 'Evaluation Material' creates contradiction. The document later refers back to the 'Receiving Party', creating confusion on who the document is referring to, since at the beggining was defined as 'Claiming Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: July 1, 2005 Page 2 of 8 The term “Evaluation Material” shall also be deemed to include any oral, written or visual information obtained by meeting Representatives of the Disclosing Party or touring any of its facilities. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, (ii) was within your possession prior to its being furnished to you by or on behalf of the Company; provided that the source of such information was not known to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Company or any other party with respect to such information, (iii) was developed independently by the Receiving Party without use, directly or indirectly, of any Evaluation Material or (iv) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, provided that such source was not known to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Company or any other party with respect to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Evaluation Material' in relation to the 'Receiving Party'. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Claiming Party'. The contradicted text refers back to the 'Receiving Party' and their obligations concerning 'Evaluation Material'. This creates a direct contradiction because the document now uses two different terms ('Receiving Party' and 'Claiming Party') to potentially refer to the same entity in the context of 'Evaluation Material', causing confusion and uncertainty about who is responsible for the obligations related to the 'Evaluation Material', which fits the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise permitted hereunder, the Receiving Party hereby agrees that the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose, that the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information (as defined below) will be kept confidential and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise permitted hereunder, the Claiming Party hereby agrees that the Claiming Party and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose, that the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information (as defined below) will be kept confidential and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.explanation: Using 'Claiming Party' at the beggining of the sentence, and 'Receiving Party' at the end of it, creates a contradiction. This is because the sentence starts reffering to the Claiming Party's duties and responsabilities, but ends up assigning them to the Receiving Party, creating confusion of who has to do what.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise permitted hereunder, the Claiming Party hereby agrees that the Claiming Party and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose, that the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information (as defined below) will be kept confidential and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information to such of its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of assisting the Receiving Party in its evaluation of a Possible Transaction, are provided with a copy of this letter agreement, agree to keep the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information confidential and agree to be bound by the terms of this letter agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text correctly uses 'Receiving Party' consistently, outlining their responsibilities. The changed text introduces 'Claiming Party' at the beginning but retains 'Receiving Party' later in the same sentence. This creates a direct contradiction because it assigns the same obligations to two different parties within the same clause. The contradicted text also uses 'Claiming Party' and 'Receiving Party' assigning different responsabilities to each. Therefore, the change creates a clear contradiction that would lead to confusion about who is responsible for the confidentiality and use of the Evaluation Material, which aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In any event, the Receiving Party agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, the Receiving Party hereby accepts responsibility for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy that the Company may have against such Representatives), and the Receiving Party agrees at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In any event, the Claiming Party agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, the Receiving Party hereby accepts responsibility for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy that the Company may have against such Representatives), and the Claiming Party agrees at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information.
perturbation.explanation: Switching between 'Claiming Party' and 'Receiving Party' within the same sentence blurs responsibilities. It's unclear who is ultimately responsible for breaches of the agreement. This ambiguity weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any event, the Claiming Party agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, the Receiving Party hereby accepts responsibility for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy that the Company may have against such Representatives), and the Claiming Party agrees at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Claiming Party' in the modified text, while the other party remains as 'Receiving Party,' directly contradicts the original assignment of responsibilities outlined in the contradicted text. This creates ambiguity regarding who is responsible for breaches and for restraining representatives, as both parties are now assigned conflicting roles in the same sentence, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, the Receiving Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to the Receiving Party and July 1, 2005 Page 3 of 8 its Representatives, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof and the identity of the parties thereto (collectively, the “ Discussion Information ”); provided that, in the event the Receiving Party becomes obligated to make public disclosure of Discussion Information pursuant to its obligations under 1934 Act or any other applicable law, rule or regulation, the Receiving Party shall, except as otherwise consented to by the Disclosing Party, limit such disclosure to the minimum disclosure so required and, without limiting the foregoing, to the extent the Receiving Party has any discretion with respect thereto, the Receiving Party shall not disclose any possible terms or of any possible transaction involving the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, the Receiving Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Claiming Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to the Receiving Party and July 1, 2005 Page 3 of 8 its Representatives, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof and the identity of the parties thereto (collectively, the “ Discussion Information ”); provided that, in the event the Claiming Party becomes obligated to make public disclosure of Discussion Information pursuant to its obligations under 1934 Act or any other applicable law, rule or regulation, the Receiving Party shall, except as otherwise consented to by the Disclosing Party, limit such disclosure to the minimum disclosure so required and, without limiting the foregoing, to the extent the Claiming Party has any discretion with respect thereto, the Receiving Party shall not disclose any possible terms or of any possible transaction involving the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The sentence mixes 'Claiming Party' and 'Receiving Party,' which muddies who is bound by the non-disclosure obligations, especially regarding the Evaluation Material and Discussion Information. It creates a contradiction to who should be held accountable if the Evaluation Material has been disclosed by any of the parties
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, the Receiving Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Claiming Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to the Receiving Party and July 1, 2005 Page 3 of 8 its Representatives, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof and the identity of the parties thereto (collectively, the “ Discussion Information ”); provided that, in the event the Claiming Party becomes obligated to make public disclosure of Discussion Information pursuant to its obligations under 1934 Act or any other applicable law, rule or regulation, the Receiving Party shall, except as otherwise consented to by the Disclosing Party, limit such disclosure to the minimum disclosure so required and, without limiting the foregoing, to the extent the Claiming Party has any discretion with respect thereto, the Receiving Party shall not disclose any possible terms or of any possible transaction involving the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Claiming Party' in a sentence that outlines the Receiving Party's obligations regarding non-disclosure. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the issue lies in the modification itself. By mixing 'Claiming Party' and 'Receiving Party' within the same obligation, it becomes unclear who is responsible for maintaining confidentiality, creating a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction as it muddies the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if the Evaluation Material were used or disclosed except as authorized by this letter. In the event that either party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, such party will promptly inform the Company of that decision. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Receiving Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly (and in no event later than five business days after the request therefor) deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Evaluation Material and Discussion Information (and any copies thereof) and cause its Representatives to do the same and the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with written confirmation of destruction; provided that outside counsel to the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in confidential restricted access files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the parties hereunder and only if reasonably related to such dispute.
perturbation.changed_text: The Claiming Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if the Evaluation Material were used or disclosed except as authorized by this letter. In the event that either party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, such party will promptly inform the Company of that decision. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Claiming Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly (and in no event later than five business days after the request therefor) deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Evaluation Material and Discussion Information (and any copies thereof) and cause its Representatives to do the same and the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with written confirmation of destruction; provided that outside counsel to the Claiming Party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in confidential restricted access files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the parties hereunder and only if reasonably related to such dispute.
perturbation.explanation: Changing between 'Receiving Party' and 'Claiming Party' creates a contradiction regarding document destruction responsibilities. It introduces confusion over who has the specific duty to destroy the materials, especially considering the 'Receiving Party' is defined at the begging of the document as the 'Claiming Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Claiming Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if the Evaluation Material were used or disclosed except as authorized by this letter. In the event that either party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, such party will promptly inform the Company of that decision. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Claiming Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly (and in no event later than five business days after the request therefor) deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Evaluation Material and Discussion Information (and any copies thereof) and cause its Representatives to do the same and the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with written confirmation of destruction; provided that outside counsel to the Claiming Party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in confidential restricted access files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the parties hereunder and only if reasonably related to such dispute.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Claiming Party' in one location but not another within the same paragraph. The paragraph now states "Claiming Party shall...provide the Disclosing Party with written confirmation of destruction; provided that outside counsel to the Receiving Party may retain one copy...", contradicting itself by assigning different roles to different parties. Given the initial definition of 'Claiming Party' as the 'Receiving Party', this creates a clear contradiction and falls under the Misaligned Terminology definition. The contradicted text is essentially the changed text itself in this case. The original text did not have this contradiction.
